kern/syscall/open_syscall.c

Go to the documentation of this file.
00001 /*
00002  * open_syscall.c
00003  *
00004  *  Created on: May 16, 2010
00005  *      Author: Luigi Grimaudo  153740
00006  *                      Piera Gueli             160119
00007  */
00008 
00009 #include <types.h>
00010 #include <kern/errno.h>
00011 #include <kern/reboot.h>
00012 #include <kern/unistd.h>
00013 #include <lib.h>
00014 #include <spl.h>
00015 #include <clock.h>
00016 #include <thread.h>
00017 #include <current.h>
00018 #include <synch.h>
00019 #include <vm.h>
00020 #include <mainbus.h>
00021 #include <vfs.h>
00022 #include <device.h>
00023 #include <syscall.h>
00024 #include <kern/fcntl.h>
00025 #include <kern/file.h>
00026 
00027 /*
00028  * Open opens the file, device, or other kernel object named by the pathname
00029  * filename. The flags argument specifies how to open the file. The optional
00030  * mode argument is only meaningful in Unix (or if you choose to implement
00031  * Unix-style security later on) and can be ignored.
00032  *
00033  */
00034 
00035 int sys_open(const char* filename, int flags, int* retval)
00036 {
00037 
00038         int vfs_result;
00039         int errno;
00040         int fd;
00041         struct file* new_file_entry;
00042         struct vnode* tmp_node;
00043 
00044         /* Check open files table size */
00045         if (curthread->last_fd == MAX_FD)
00046         {
00047                 errno = EMFILE;
00048                 return errno;
00049         }
00050 
00051         /* Try to open file */
00052         vfs_result = vfs_open((char*)filename, flags,0, &tmp_node);
00053 
00054         if (vfs_result != 0)
00055         {
00056                 errno = vfs_result;
00057                 return errno;
00058         }
00059 
00060         /* Look for a free open files table entry */
00061         for (fd=0; fd< MAX_FD; fd++){
00062                 if ( curthread->table_fd[fd] == NULL)
00063                         break;
00064         }
00065 
00066         /* Create a file entry */
00067         new_file_entry = (struct file*) kmalloc(sizeof(struct file));
00068         if (new_file_entry == NULL)
00069         {
00070                 errno = EMFILE;
00071                 return errno;
00072         }
00073 
00074         /* Populate file entry */
00075         new_file_entry->vfs_node = tmp_node;
00076         new_file_entry->offset = 0;
00077 
00078         switch (flags & O_ACCMODE){
00079         case O_RDONLY:
00080                 new_file_entry->readable = true;
00081                 new_file_entry->writeable = false;
00082                 break;
00083         case O_WRONLY:
00084                 new_file_entry->readable = false;
00085                 new_file_entry->writeable = true;
00086                 break;
00087         case O_RDWR:
00088                 new_file_entry->readable = true;
00089                 new_file_entry->writeable = true;
00090                 break;
00091         default:
00092                 errno = EINVAL;
00093                 return errno;
00094         }
00095 
00096         /* load file entry to open files table */
00097         curthread->table_fd[fd] = new_file_entry;
00098         curthread->last_fd++;
00099 
00100 
00101         /* Return value to user */
00102         *retval = fd;
00103 
00104         return 0;
00105 }
